# Novelty Audit — DualWeaver (c29f9255)

**The novel axis is the dual-surrogate paradigm: two structurally symmetric surrogate series generated by a shared feature-fusion module enable non-parametric reconstruction of multivariate predictions from univariate TSFMs, avoiding the capacity loss of encoder-decoder approaches.**

## Decomposition

- **Native multivariate TSFMs (prior art):** Moirai (Woo et al. 2024) flattens all channels simultaneously (quadratic memory cost); GTT uses shared-weight cross-variable attention; Chronos-2 applies sequential temporal+variate attention. DualWeaver is positioned as a lightweight adapter over existing Uni-TSFMs, not a standalone TSFM.
- **Dual surrogate paradigm (the novel axis):** Rather than an encoder mapping to latent + decoder, DualWeaver uses a pair of symmetric surrogates that are (a) TSFM-compatible (1D series) and (b) enriched with cross-variable dependencies via a shared feature-fusion module. Non-parametric reconstruction from surrogates eliminates the encoder-decoder capacity bottleneck. This architectural choice is specific and not obviously equivalent to prior adapter approaches.
- **Theoretical error bound (adds rigor):** The error analysis under surrogate mapping grounds the non-parametric reconstruction claim.

## Reframe

The contribution is most accurately framed as an "adapter paradigm for Uni-TSFMs that captures cross-variable dependencies via dual symmetric surrogates without any capacity loss from encoding." The "synergistic feature weaving" title is slightly obscure; "dual surrogate adapter" would be more precise.

## Concrete fix

A comparison with a simpler adapter baseline (e.g., a single surrogate vs. dual) would isolate the benefit of the symmetric dual structure specifically. Without this, it's unclear whether the gain comes from the dual symmetry or from the cross-variable feature fusion module alone.
